description: Closeout PRD for F-003 (Pro Billing + Tier Gating Verification). The original F-003 PRD covers the full verification surface; this closeout PRD contains only the work still open as of 2026-05-04. It groups the remaining items into atomic, ordered stories and treats them as an independent shippable bundle. Highest-leverage item is US-002 (write `api/services/claude_service.py`), which unblocks the 33 Pro-gated interactive routes — the headline 'Pro features' the pricing page advertises. The remaining stories are verification, cleanup, and operator-gated end-to-end round-trips.

commits_today: 7b60315 feat(billing): 14-day trial on Pro/Team Checkout for first-time customers
a09aaec fix(auth): wire password reset email through Resend
6d3b173 refactor(auth): delegate password reset to Auth0 universal login (ADR-0002)
sigil_infra_prs_today: #2 fix(tf): declare AUTH0 + GitHub OAuth env vars in TF
#3 fix(tf): declare Stripe Price IDs in CI tfvars

userStories.title: Decide claude_service shape: shim vs refactor vs drop-bulk
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a documented decision (ADR-0003) on how to provide the missing `api/services/claude_service.py` so that downstream stories implement against an agreed contract — not against three open branches.
acceptanceCriteria: ADR-0003 exists at docs/adr/ADR-0003-claude-service-strategy.md with `status: accepted` after owner approval
ADR enumerates the three branches from F1.7-BLOCKED.md (a) thin shim over LLMService, (b) refactor bulk_analyzer to use LLMService directly, (c) drop bulk-analyze endpoints from interactive.py
ADR records chosen branch + rationale + reversibility
Verification: `test -f docs/adr/ADR-0003-claude-service-strategy.md && grep -q '^status: accepted' docs/adr/ADR-0003-claude-service-strategy.md` exits 0

userStories.title: Implement claude_service per ADR-0003 + unblock interactive.router
userStories.description: As a paying Pro customer, I want the 33 interactive routes (AI Investigation, False Positive Verification, Interactive Chat, Attack Chain, Compliance Mapping) to load so that the features I'm paying for actually exist at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Branch chosen in ADR-0003 is implemented (shim, refactor, or route-removal)
`python3 -c 'from api.routers import interactive; assert interactive.router is not None'` exits 0
Removed both `@pytest.mark.skip` decorators in `api/tests/test_interactive_router_registered.py`
`python3 -m pytest api/tests/test_interactive_router_registered.py -v` shows 2 PASSED, 0 SKIPPED
`api/main.py` imports `interactive.router` and registers it via `app.include_router(...)`
`curl -sS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -X POST https://api.sigilsec.ai/v1/interactive/investigate -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}'` returns 401 or 422 (auth/validation), NOT 404
files: api/services/claude_service.py (or removed depending on ADR branch)

userStories.title: Test-mode Stripe webhook subscription audit + fix
userStories.description: As the launch operator, I want test-mode Stripe to fire all six required events to `https://api.sigilsec.ai/v1/billing/webhook` so that STORY-105 can run end-to-end against a real Stripe Checkout test card.
acceptanceCriteria: evidence/F-003/US-105a-test-mode-webhook-audit.md captures the test-mode webhook endpoint ID + the verbatim list of `enabled_events`

If event list is missing any of the 6 required events (checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.created/updated/deleted, invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed), POST the same fix as STORY-101 against the test-mode endpoint and re-verify
Re-verify GET on the test-mode endpoint: `count: 6` containing all 6 required events
Verification: evidence file exists + final GET shows 6/6 events

userStories.title: Stripe Dashboard test-send positive control — close STORY-102
userStories.description: As the launch operator, I want to confirm the live-mode webhook handler returns 200 to a Dashboard test-send so that we know the signing secret on prod matches the configured endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: Operator triggers a Dashboard test-send on the live-mode webhook endpoint (`we_1T2AXKFhPhxEz27fCYP53mKc`)
Container log for `sigil-api` shows the corresponding event reaching `/v1/billing/webhook` with HTTP 200 within 30s
evidence/F-003/US-102-webhook-signature-roundtrip.md updated with positive-control section: event ID + container log timestamp + 200 verdict
Verification: grep '^## Positive Control' evidence/F-003/US-102-webhook-signature-roundtrip.md returns content

userStories.title: Test-mode end-to-end round-trip — close STORY-105
userStories.description: As the launch operator, I want a 12-section evidence file capturing the full Free → Pro → cancel loop in Stripe TEST mode so that we know every gate, webhook, and DB update works without spending real money.
acceptanceCriteria: evidence/F-003/US-105-testmode-roundtrip.md exists with all 12 sections per existing STORY-105 spec (Auth0 signup, MSSQL T0=free, 403 baseline, real checkout.stripe.com URL, 4242 card completion, customer.subscription.created event ID, container log 200 within 30s, MSSQL T1=pro with stripe_customer_id+stripe_subscription_id populated, Pro endpoint 200 with real LLM body, /v1/billing/portal returns billing.stripe.com URL, customer.subscription.deleted event ID from portal cancel, MSSQL T2=free + Pro endpoint 403)
All test card numbers are Stripe TEST cards (4242 4242 4242 4242)
Webhook timing: each event reflects in MSSQL within 30s
Verification: `wc -l evidence/F-003/US-105-testmode-roundtrip.md` returns ≥ 200 lines AND each of 12 sections has a `## Section N` header

userStories.title: Trialing-state verification — close STORY-107 Branch C
userStories.description: As the launch operator, I want the test-mode round-trip from US-005 to capture `subscription.status='trialing'` rows so that ADR-0001 (14-day trial) graduates from `outcome: pending` to `outcome: success`.
acceptanceCriteria: During US-005 execution, MSSQL T1 row for the test-mode subscription has `status='trialing'` (NOT `active`) immediately after checkout completion
Stripe webhook event payload includes `trial_end` timestamp ~14 days in the future
evidence/F-003/US-107-trialing-state-verification.md captures the verbatim row and webhook payload snippet
ADR-0001 frontmatter updated: `outcome: success`, `outcome_date: <today>`, with a one-line note linking to evidence
Verification: `grep -E '^outcome: success' docs/adr/ADR-0001-stripe-free-trial.md` exits 0

userStories.title: Delete dead create-checkout route — close STORY-106
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the unreferenced `dashboard/src/app/api/billing/create-checkout/route.ts` deleted so that no production code path can return a fabricated `cs_test_<tier>_<cycle>_<ts>` URL.
acceptanceCriteria: `grep -rn '/api/billing/create-checkout' dashboard/src` returns ZERO matches (exits 1)
`test -e dashboard/src/app/api/billing/create-checkout/route.ts` returns false (file deleted)
`pnpm --dir dashboard build` exits 0 (no missing import errors)
evidence/F-003/US-106-dead-route-removed.md records pre/post grep + build output
Verification: build passes + grep is empty

userStories.title: Live-mode end-to-end round-trip with one real $29 charge + refund — close STORY-109
userStories.description: As the launch operator (owner-only), I want one real Pro Checkout completed with a real card, the resulting Stripe invoice paid for $29.00, then refunded within 24h — proving end-to-end live-mode operation with documented refund safety.
acceptanceCriteria: evidence/F-003/US-109-livemode-roundtrip.md contains the same 12 sections as US-005 (live-mode, real card)
Section 13: Stripe invoice `paid:true, amount:2900, currency:usd, livemode:true`
Section 14: refund event ID + Stripe response showing `status: succeeded, amount: 2900`
Section 15: MSSQL T3 row shows subscription_tier reverted (free) post-refund
Refund initiated within 24 hours of charge
Verification: 15-section evidence file + Stripe invoice + Stripe refund both visible in dashboard
userStories.irreversible_action: real $29 charge — owner explicit go-ahead required per Auto-Mode rule 5

userStories.title: Terraform-import 5 stale monitor_metric_alert resources
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the 5 `azurerm_monitor_metric_alert` resources that exist in Azure but are not in TF state to be imported so that every CI Apply step stops failing on these out-of-band resources.
acceptanceCriteria: `terraform import` succeeds for all 5: `azurerm_monitor_metric_alert.api_response_time`, `azurerm_monitor_metric_alert.api_replicas_down`, `azurerm_monitor_metric_alert.api_high_cpu`, `azurerm_monitor_metric_alert.dashboard_replicas_down`, `azurerm_monitor_metric_alert.redis_down`
Subsequent `terraform plan` shows zero `to be created` for these 5 resources (state matches reality)
Next CI Apply on a sigil-infra change exits 0 (or only fails for unrelated reasons)
evidence/F-003/US-009-monitor-alert-imports.md captures the 5 import commands + plan diff before/after
Verification: most recent sigil-infra workflow run after the imports exits 0 on the Apply job
files: (no code changes — state-only operation in sigil-infra)

userStories.title: Auth0 test-user cleanup
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the 3 test users created during the agent-browser round-trip on 2026-05-03 deleted from the Auth0 tenant so that prod identity store is hygienic.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth0 Dashboard → User Management shows zero users matching the test-email pattern from `evidence/F-003/US-104-105-agent-browser-roundtrip.md`
MSSQL `users` table — corresponding rows either deleted or marked `email LIKE '%@test.invalid'` (whichever the operator prefers)
evidence/F-003/US-010-auth0-cleanup.md records the user IDs deleted (no PII beyond Auth0 user_id)
Verification: Auth0 dashboard search by domain returns 0 results
userStories.note: Defer until US-005 done — those test users are still needed for the round-trip evidence

Owner gates marked: US-001 (ADR approval), US-004 (operator action), US-005 (owner-supervised checkout), US-008 (real-money charge), US-009 (state surgery), US-010 (identity cleanup)
